title: Annotation & AI Colab System: Engineering Brief
description: A comprehensive technical brief for stakeholders explaining the new human annotation and AI Colab features in the Visualizer.

mutations.effect: Start a new thread anchored to a component
Edit a message you authored; updates updatedAt
Delete a message; removes the thread if empty

props.text: The Visualizer now supports two complementary collaboration modes directly on any rendered artifact: persistent human comments anchored to components, and an ephemeral AI Colab mode that formats the artifact plus in-memory notes into ready-to-paste Markdown for an LLM.

props.label: Mutation types
props.caption: create, reply, resolve, reopen, edit, delete

props.title: Two modes, one goal
props.description: Both features let a reviewer leave context on a rendered artifact, but they differ in lifecycle, storage, and output.

props.content:
**Human annotations** are persistent, threaded discussions anchored to artifact nodes. They are saved to a local `annotations.json` file via the CLI server, support open/resolved status, and include author identity from git config.

**AI Colab** is an ephemeral, in-memory mode. Reviewers attach notes to components or to the whole artifact, choose a Markdown detail level (compact / summary / full), and copy a formatted prompt to the clipboard. No file is written, no LLM is called, and the notes disappear when the artifact changes or the page is refreshed.

props.title: Human annotation data flow
props.description: How a user comment becomes a persisted thread.

props.title: Human annotation request path
props.code:
sequenceDiagram
    actor U as User
    participant R as VisualArtifactRenderer
    participant P as AnnotationProvider
    participant UI as AnnotationPanel
    participant S as CLI server /api/annotations
    participant D as annotations.json

    U->>R: click a component
    R->>P: selectNodeForComment(node)
    P->>UI: open node composer
    U->>UI: type comment + Post
    UI->>P: createThread(anchor, body)
    P->>S: POST mutations [{createThread}]
    S->>S: applyMutations(doc, mutations)
    S->>D: write file
    S-->>P: updated document
    P->>UI: thread list / thread detail

props.title: Annotation domain model
AI Colab data flow
props.description: How a reviewer builds a prompt from the artifact plus in-memory notes.

props.title: AI Colab copy path
props.code:
sequenceDiagram
    actor U as User
    participant SH as SiteHeader (ToggleGroup)
    participant P as AIColabProvider
    participant UI as AIColabPanel
    participant F as formatter.ts
    participant C as Clipboard

    U->>SH: toggle Colab
    SH->>P: openAIColab()
    P->>UI: show panel (desktop) or pick HUD (mobile)
    U->>UI: add artifact/node comments
    U->>UI: choose Compact / Summary / Full
    U->>UI: Copy Markdown
    UI->>P: copyToClipboard()
    P->>F: formatArtifactForAI(spec, { comments, level })
    F-->>P: Markdown string
    P->>C: navigator.clipboard.writeText(markdown)

props.title: AI Colab comment model
Example formatted Markdown output
Annotation panel states
props.code:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Closed
    Closed --> Picking : startNodePick
    Picking --> List : selectNodeForComment
    Closed --> List : openComments
    List --> Node : click component / selectThread
    Node --> Thread : selectThread
    Thread --> List : navigateBack
    Node --> List : navigateBack
    List --> Closed : closeComments
    Thread --> Closed : closeComments

props.title: Mutation contract
props.description: All human-annotation mutations are persisted through a single POST endpoint and applied sequentially.

props.title: Key files and responsibilities
props.description: Where each concern lives in the codebase. Tap a file to preview its source; git badges reflect the current branch state.

props.content: The system is split into four layers. **Annotation state** lives in `annotation-provider.tsx` (React context, mutations, persistence) and `annotation-panel.tsx` (thread list, message bubbles, edit/delete). **AI Colab state** mirrors that split: `ai-colab-provider.tsx` holds context + pick state, `ai-colab-panel.tsx` renders the composer + comment list, and `ai-colab-store.ts` builds targets and copies Markdown. **Formatting** is isolated in `lib/ai-colab/formatter.ts` (spec → Markdown) with its `types.ts`. **Shared contracts** — the Zod schemas for threads, messages, anchors, and mutations — live in `skill/shared/src/annotations.ts`, consumed by both the app and the CLI. The CLI's `skill/cli/src/lib/annotations.ts` applies mutations to the on-disk document. `site-header.tsx` hosts the Comments/Colab toggle group; `visual-artifact-renderer.tsx` owns node selection and overlays both panels.

props.title: Validation results
props.description: Every touched package was checked before this brief was written.

props.content:
Reviewers no longer need to leave the rendered artifact to give feedback. Human annotations create a durable, auditable record tied to the exact component being discussed. AI Colab turns the same interaction into a structured prompt workflow: the reviewer points, comments, and copies context-ready Markdown, reducing the friction of asking an LLM to act on a visual design.

Both modes reuse the same renderer, selection, and anchor infrastructure, so the marginal cost of adding either feature is low. The architecture is also extensible: a future sender can replace the clipboard step with a remote webhook or API call without changing the formatter or the in-memory comment model.
